high_value_elements: Correctly describes PBM mechanism: photons absorbed by cytochrome C oxidase in mitochondria, producing ATP, nitric oxide, and reactive oxygen species
Accurately explains locoregional mechanism: sodium channel blockade preventing action potential propagation
Correctly identifies acupuncture/electroacupuncture analgesia via endogenous opioid release and spinal cord modulation
Describes epidural analgesia as neuraxial blockade of afferent pain signals at the spinal level
Associates each technique with an appropriate primary clinical indication
major_concerns: Confuses PBM photobiomodulation with thermal/surgical laser effects
Cannot explain how any of the four technique categories produces analgesia mechanistically
States PBM works by 'heating tissue' without distinguishing photobiomodulation from photothermal effects
Cannot distinguish locoregional from systemic analgesia in mechanism

high_value_elements: Selects anatomically appropriate nerve block for TECA-BO (inferior alveolar, auriculotemporal, or auricular/mandibular blocks)
Chooses appropriate local anesthetic with rationale for concentration and volume relative to patient weight
Integrates postoperative PBM or acupuncture into a coherent multimodal plan
Applies laser parameter adjustments for a large, dark-coated dog (coat color adjustment, increased fluence)
Describes appropriate post-procedural monitoring for locoregional patients
major_concerns: Selects a locoregional technique anatomically inappropriate for the surgical site
Cannot describe or calculate maximum safe local anesthetic dose
Proposes laser as a standalone analgesic replacing pharmacological management
Ignores patient comorbidities when selecting technique

high_value_elements: Prioritizes safety screening (neoplasia, cardiac disease, open growth plates, pregnancy, coat moisture, eye protection) before any PBM session
Identifies Doberman breed risk for dilated cardiomyopathy; recommends cardiac screening before modalities using electrical current or laser near thorax
Sequences technique delivery logically within a perioperative timeline
Correctly identifies when a technique is outside personal scope and describes a safe referral pathway
major_concerns: Fails to identify neoplasia as an absolute PBM contraindication
Does not screen for cardiac disease in a Doberman before electroacupuncture or laser
Cannot identify LAST as a serious complication requiring immediate intervention
Applies laser over a wet coat without recognizing this as a dosing safety issue

high_value_elements: Provides mechanism-based rationale for nerve block selection at TECA-BO
Explains why on-contact PBM delivery produces superior tissue penetration versus off-contact
Justifies electroacupuncture for spinal/neuropathic indications with specific neurophysiologic reasoning
Explains why a dark coat requires higher fluence and how to adjust parameters
major_concerns: Makes technique selections without any supporting rationale
Relies on anecdote rather than fellowship-taught principles
Cannot explain why a chosen block is anatomically appropriate for the procedure

high_value_elements: Acknowledges scope-of-competence limits for techniques not yet trained in
Identifies PBM contraindications: neoplasia, active hemorrhage, pregnancy, eyes, open growth plates, deep active infection, active cardiac arrhythmia, pacemaker
Recognizes limits of evidence for some PBM indications while identifying well-supported uses
Describes appropriate training pathway before independently performing new locoregional techniques
major_concerns: Cannot name a single contraindication to Class IV laser therapy
Presents all techniques as equally applicable to all patients without contraindication awareness
Claims readiness for techniques clearly beyond introduction level without acknowledging skill gap

high_value_elements: Integrates multiple techniques synergistically with mechanistic rationale for each combination
Understands why synergism allows dose reduction of individual pharmacologic agents
References DM rehabilitation research (255-day vs. 55-day survival) when discussing PBM for neurological patients
Applies continuous wave vs. superpulsed distinction with clinical implications for dose delivery
major_concerns: Cannot discuss any interaction between techniques
Treats techniques as interchangeable without mechanism-based patient matching
Names techniques without understanding clinical decision points
